approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as an eligible hedged item, making the fair value hedge model a relevant top-level hedge-accounting family for FX risk on a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also treats forecast transactions and risk exposures giving rise to variability in amounts recognised in profit or loss as hedged items, so the cash flow hedge model is a peer hedge-accounting family relevant to FX exposures considered in consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations as a distinct top-level hedge-accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
